Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First summarize normative flow Always start with Receive order After receiving must check inventory before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment After inventory check two branches If inventory sufficient Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order If inventory insufficient Cancel order Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus constraints Authorize payment must be after Check inventory and before Package items Package items must be after Authorize payment and before Ship order Ship order must be after Package items and never after Cancel order Close order is final in both scenarios

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Sequence follows normative flow for sufficient inventory This matches exactly No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here packaging before authorization is a violation because the correct order is Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also Authorize payment after packaging violates normative constraints and shipping occurs after packaging but before a valid authorization sequence so case 2 is non compliant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order The presence of both Authorize payment and Cancel order is a violation because if inventory is insufficient the process should cancel after checking inventory rather than authorize payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After Cancel order they then Ship order That is not allowed The normative text says shipping should never happen after a cancellation and after cancellation the next step should be Close order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order According to normative for insufficient inventory this matches exactly No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Missing Package items According to normative packaging must occur after authorization and before shipping Here shipping occurs directly after authorization skipping packaging so this is a violation

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the cases that deviate from the intended process are Cases 2 3 and 5 Cases 1 and 4 are compliant
